highlights: Hub latest: 95b8ec9 data: autonomous standup overnight-sprint 2026-02-19-2331

cro.description: Growth strategy, monetization. Gazelle-intense focus on revenue.

cmo.description: Content, creative direction, brand. Obsessed with offers, value equations, and volume.
concerns: 1 podcast episode(s) not synced to website

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alright team, it's late Thursday night. Let me set the stage. Last sprint we committed to shipping the privacy policy template and drafting App Store listing assets. We've got 721 commits in seven days — build is healthy. The critical path remains unchanged: get Lever into the App Store. Three critical sync gaps still open, one uncommitted hub change. Let's hear where we actually landed and what we can close in the next fifteen minutes. Elon, start us off. Elon (CTO): Privacy policy template got drafted and committed — that's in the hub now. The one uncommitted file you're seeing is a minor data artifact from tonight's standup logging, not blocking anything. On the three critical sync gaps — podcast MDX mismatch, blog rendering, and App Store submission — podcast and blog are content pipeline issues, not engineering blockers. The real engineering bottleneck is the App Store submission itself. We need those listing assets finalized so I can package the build and push it through Xcode. Seven TODOs and FIXMEs in the codebase — none are submission blockers, all cosmetic.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: The listing assets are what matter right now. App name, subtitle, description, keywords, and screenshots. That's the entire surface area a user sees before they decide to download. We need to nail the positioning in under thirty words for the subtitle. Something like "Finance, Faith & Family — Your Daily Lever." Screenshots need to show all four tabs with real data, not placeholder content. If we ship mediocre screenshots, we've wasted every hour we put into the product.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: I agree with Steve on the screenshots being make-or-break. For the description copy, I can draft the full App Store listing — long description, promotional text, keyword set — in this sprint cycle. On content sync, A-012 is still open: we have one podcast episode that hasn't made it to the website. That's a quick win I can close tonight alongside the listing draft. Faith lesson content is at seven of our fifteen to twenty target but the CEO already decided we ship with seven, so that's not blocking submission.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Revenue reality check — we have zero active revenue streams and the single biggest unlock is getting this app live. Every day we're not in the App Store is a day we can't even begin measuring conversion or building an audience funnel. The free launch with a designed paid tier ready to toggle — that's the right call. I want to make sure we're not gold-plating the listing when "good enough to submit" gets us into the review queue days faster. Cash position is stable at seventy-five-three, Fear and Greed still at 39, no action needed per CEO directive.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: From a measurement standpoint, we should ensure we have basic analytics instrumented before submission. If we push this to the App Store without event tracking on tab switches, lesson completions, and session duration, we'll be flying blind on what users actually engage with. That's a small lift — probably already partially in place — but worth confirming before we submit.

transcript.speaker: Chris Vermeullen
transcript.message: Market context is sideways with fear sentiment. Paper portfolio sitting at just under a hundred K, four positions held, no buy signals tonight. Cash stays liquid per Joshua's directive. Nothing to act on from the financial tools side. The paid tier design Dave mentioned — when that conversation happens, I'd want to model the pricing against our user acquisition assumptions.

transcript.speaker: faith
transcript.message: Jett's vaccine appointment is tomorrow, February 20th. That reminder is live and tracked. The seven faith lessons cover all eight dimensions, which gives at least some content in every area for launch. Solid foundation to build on post-submission.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: James raises a fair point on analytics, but I'd push back on making it a submission blocker. We can ship a point release with enhanced tracking in week one. Apple review takes days — we can instrument during the review window. Elon (CTO): Dave's right. Analytics is a day-two patch, not a gate. I'll flag it as a post-submission task. The actual blocker right now is the listing assets — once Alex has those drafted, I can package everything and we submit.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: Agreed on not blocking for analytics, but James — make sure we at least have crash reporting active. That's non-negotiable before we put this in front of real users.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good. Clear alignment. Here's where we land for this sprint. Critical path is unchanged — App Store submission. The three existing ecosystem sync gaps under A-001 and A-010 remain our highest priority work for Elon to close. The five attention items under A-002 get reviewed in parallel. Dave continues monitoring the cash position under J-001. One decision for Joshua: we need to confirm whether the listing assets draft is good enough to submit or if he wants a review pass first. Let's ship.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Joshua wants a review pass on the listing before we submit. That's our one new gate on the critical path. Alex: finish the full listing draft — name, subtitle, description, keywords, screenshot specs — and queue it for Joshua tonight. Joshua reviews, gives the green light, Elon packages and submits. Steve confirms crash reporting is active before the build is packaged. Analytics instrumentation is a post-submission task per team consensus. Content sync gaps and the seven TODOs run in parallel but do not block. Jett's vaccine appointment tomorrow is tracked and confirmed. Clean sprint. Let's ship.

actionItems.text: Draft full App Store listing: app name, subtitle, description, promotional text, keywords, and screenshot specifications
Review and approve App Store listing draft before submission
Confirm crash reporting is active and functional in the current build
Package build and submit to App Store once Joshua approves listing
Close podcast MDX sync gap and blog rendering issues (A-001, A-010) in parallel
Close A-012: publish missing podcast episode to website
Instrument analytics (tab switches, lesson completions, session duration) as post-submission patch during Apple review window
Continue monitoring cash position and market signals per J-001 directive
Jett vaccine appointment tomorrow February 20 — tracked and confirmed

topPriorities: Alex delivers complete App Store listing draft (copy + screenshot brief) to Joshua for review
Joshua reviews and approves listing assets to unblock submission
Elon packages final build and submits to App Store immediately upon Joshua's approval

ceoDirectives.directive: What is Lever's direction? → Hybrid — personal tool now with product optionality built in
Should faith content integrate into the Family tab? → Keep faith content as separate standalone section
Deploy idle cash ($75,340)? → No action — keep cash liquid
How deep should standalone faith content go? → Medium build: 15-20 lessons with 2-3 per dimension
What is the minimum viable scope for the Lever App Store submission? → Full current build: all 4 tabs (Finance, Faith, Family, Compass) as-is with 7 faith lessons
Should we unify the dual Supabase instances (Hub vs Website) this sprint or defer? → Defer until after App Store submission — live with manual sync for now
Do we have a privacy policy and terms of service URL ready for the App Store submission? → Need to draft and host tonight — use a simple template
Should we define a paid tier structure before or after the App Store launch? → Launch free but have the paid tier designed and ready to toggle on

Should we prioritize drafting the privacy policy and App Store listing assets (screenshots, description, keywords) THIS sprint as the critical path to submission, or defer to tomorrow? → Ship privacy policy template and draft listing assets tonight

ceoDecisions.question: Should we submit the App Store listing as soon as the draft is ready, or do you want a review pass before we hit submit?
ceoDecisions.answerText: Queue draft for Joshua review before submitting
options: Submit immediately once draft is complete
Queue draft for Joshua review before submitting
Submit with placeholder text and update during review window
